seoTitle: How to Change Your Zendesk Email Signature
metaDescription: Learn how to change your Zendesk signature, whether editing your personal agent profile or updating the global template as an administrator.
intro: An outdated signature with broken links or generic footer text can make support replies look unpolished. Zendesk lets agents edit their personal signature and lets admins update a shared global template using Markdown formatting. This guide covers both routes.

steps.heading: Open your agent profile
steps.body: Click into your user profile from the top navigation to access your personal signature settings.

steps.heading: Edit your signature block
steps.body: Locate the signature box in the left sidebar and rewrite your name, title, and contact details.

steps.heading: Apply Markdown formatting
steps.body: Insert Markdown syntax for bold text, italics, or clickable links to style your signature cleanly.

steps.heading: Update the global template
steps.body: As an admin, open the Agent Interface settings in Admin Center to edit the common signature card used across all agents.

steps.heading: Preserve dynamic placeholders
steps.body: Keep existing placeholder tokens intact when editing the global template so agent-specific data still populates correctly.

steps.heading: Test the output
steps.body: Save your changes and open a public test ticket to confirm the signature spacing and links display correctly.

faq.question: Can individual agents override the global signature?
faq.answer: Yes, personal signature edits in an agent's profile typically take precedence over the shared global template.

faq.question: Can I add an image or logo to my signature?
faq.answer: Yes, you can source an externally hosted image URL, but keep it lightweight so it doesn't break email viewport rules.

faq.question: Why do placeholder tokens matter in the global template?
faq.answer: They let each agent's name and details populate dynamically instead of hardcoding one person's info for the whole team.

faq.question: How do I confirm my signature looks right before it goes live?
faq.answer: Send or preview a public test comment to check formatting, spacing, and link behavior.
